skill.name: treatment-plans
skill.description: Create concise medical treatment plan documents from structured clinical details using LaTeX templates, SMART goals, intervention guidance, compliance references, and validation scripts.
skill.summary: Draft clinician-focused treatment plans with templates, goals, timelines, and quality checks.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 499 findings and a critical combination heuristic, but review found no prompt injection, credential exfiltration, hidden network client, or malicious code path. Most weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from medical terminology, LaTeX markup, and documentation. Real risks are moderate: Bash-enabled setup guidance, sudo package commands, local file writes, and an external AI diagram workflow that could expose clinical details if users include identifiers.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Enabled Workflow Includes Privileged Installation Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill declares Bash support and documentation includes sudo and TeX package installation commands. These are legitimate setup examples, but they can modify system TeX installations if a user follows them without review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are plainly present and privileged, but they are documented installation steps rather than hidden execution or exfiltration logic.

medium_findings.title: Local File Write and Copy Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE. Helper scripts copy templates and write timeline outputs to user-selected paths. This is expected functionality, but it creates local filesystem modification risk and possible overwrite risk if paths are chosen carelessly.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes and copy operation are direct evidence. The scripts do not use shell execution and include an overwrite prompt for template generation, so severity is moderate.

medium_findings.title: External AI Figure Workflow May Expose Clinical Details
medium_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The skill instructs users to create AI-generated clinical schematics through a separate scientific-schematics workflow and mentions Nano Banana Pro. No network code is included here, but users could send clinical details to an external service if they follow this workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external AI workflow is explicitly described, but the package does not include the referenced generator or direct network implementation to inspect.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are Mostly Clinical Terminology False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. Many high and low static alerts are triggered by medical acronyms, clinical scales, LaTeX package metadata, color values, and documentation text. I found no evidence that these locations implement cryptography, reconnaissance, credential collection, or malicious discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative flagged lines are clinical or LaTeX documentation content, not executable security-sensitive logic. The same pattern explains the repeated alerts across templates and references.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Documentation Reference
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE. The single hardcoded URL points users to CTAN for optional LaTeX package research. It is not used by code, and I found no request library, socket use, or automated outbound network call in the scripts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears only in prose documentation. A targeted search found no direct HTTP client usage in the included Python scripts.

low_findings.title: Referenced Schematic Script Is Missing
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW. The skill tells users to run scripts/generate_schematic.py, but that file is not included in the scanned package. This is primarily a reliability and marketplace accuracy issue, not confirmed malicious behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command references a specific local file, and the scanned file structure does not include it. The absence does not prove security harm, but users may be directed to an unavailable workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privileged Shell Commands in User-Facing Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The package includes sudo commands for TeX installation and package updates. Users should review these commands and prefer user-local installation when possible.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact privileged commands are present in bash blocks. They are setup instructions, not stealth execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path-Based Local Output Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: Scripts accept output paths and write generated templates or timelines. This is normal tool behavior but should be run in a controlled working directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write paths are visible in code and are part of intended output generation. No shell interpolation or remote transfer was found.

content.value_statement: Clinical teams need concise treatment plans that organize goals, interventions, monitoring, and follow-up. This skill provides templates and checks for structured, professional plan drafting.
actual_capabilities: Guides creation of one-page and multi-page treatment plans for several clinical contexts.
Provides LaTeX templates for general medical, rehabilitation, mental health, chronic disease, perioperative, and pain plans.
Uses SMART goal guidance for measurable short-term and long-term care objectives.
Includes reference material for documentation standards, interventions, compliance, and specialty-specific guidance.
Provides scripts to generate templates, check completeness, validate quality, and create timelines.
Supports PDF-oriented workflows with professional LaTeX styling guidance.
limitations: It does not replace clinical judgment, licensed medical review, or local policy approval.
Clinical guidelines and regulatory requirements may change and must be verified before use.
The referenced schematic generation workflow depends on another skill and a missing local script.
Users must remove protected health information before sharing drafts or using external AI services.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Chronic Disease Plan
use_cases.description: Draft a concise plan with diagnoses, medication actions, monitoring targets, SMART goals, and follow-up milestones.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Rehabilitation Goals
use_cases.description: Create a therapy plan that links functional baselines, visit frequency, measurable outcomes, and reassessment dates.

use_cases.title: Review Plan Completeness
use_cases.description: Check whether a draft includes required sections, measurable goals, evidence references, safety items, and patient education.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a one-page treatment plan for a de-identified adult patient with the following diagnosis, current medications, goals, and monitoring needs.

prompt_templates.title: Build SMART Goals
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these broad care goals into SMART treatment goals with measurable targets, timeframes, responsible parties, and monitoring methods.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt a Specialty Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the rehabilitation treatment plan structure to draft a concise plan from this de-identified assessment, therapy frequency, precautions, and discharge goals.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Draft Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this de-identified treatment plan for completeness, SMART goals, safety monitoring, evidence support, compliance gaps, and unnecessary detail.

output_examples.input: A de-identified patient has type 2 diabetes, elevated HbA1c, medication adherence barriers, and needs follow-up monitoring.
output_examples.output: A concise plan with diagnosis summary, treatment goals, medication actions, lifestyle supports, lab monitoring, safety thresholds, and follow-up timing.

output_examples.input: A rehabilitation patient needs a post-operative mobility plan with precautions and progress milestones.
output_examples.output: A therapy-focused plan with baseline function, visit cadence, precautions, measurable mobility goals, home program tasks, and reassessment checkpoints.

output_examples.input: A draft pain management plan needs a quality review before clinician approval.
output_examples.output: A checklist-style review noting missing safety items, unclear outcome measures, monitoring gaps, and sections that can be shortened.

best_practices: Use de-identified clinical details and verify all recommendations with a qualified clinician.
Keep plans concise, measurable, and tied to patient priorities and monitoring thresholds.
Validate local requirements for privacy, billing, consent, documentation, and specialty guidelines.
anti_patterns: Do not include direct patient identifiers in prompts, templates, figures, or example documents.
Do not treat generated medication, dosing, or diagnosis text as final medical advice.
Do not run sudo, package installation, or LaTeX commands without reviewing the command and environment.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose or prescribe treatment?
faq.answer: No. It helps draft documentation and must be reviewed by qualified clinical professionals.

faq.question: Does it support different care settings?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes templates for medical, rehabilitation, mental health, chronic disease, perioperative, and pain management plans.

faq.question: Can it generate PDF-ready documents?
faq.answer: Yes. The templates are LaTeX-based and can be compiled with a local LaTeX toolchain.

faq.question: Is protected health information safe to include?
faq.answer: No. Use de-identified information unless your environment and workflow are approved for protected health information.

faq.question: What checks are included?
faq.answer: The scripts can check completeness, quality criteria, SMART goals, measurable targets, and timeline information.

faq.question: Does the schematic workflow work by itself?
faq.answer: Not fully. The skill references a separate schematic workflow and a script that is not included in this package.
